感觉到了吗？当任天堂娱乐系统35周年纪念日悄悄逼近你时，你的血液中流淌着对你虚度的青春的无可辩驳和压倒性的怀旧之情。最初的NES明天就35岁了，为了庆祝另一个令人震惊的提醒，我们自己的死亡，乐高刚刚宣布了另一个看起来很棒的集合，计划在今年夏天晚些时候到达。
经过短暂的试探之后，NES构建套件现在正式正式发布，它配备了一个可构建的控制台、磁带盒和控制器(甚至RCA端口都在系统一侧)。游戏可以加载到系统中并锁定到位，控制器有自己的电缆，可以直接插入前面。然而，最重要的是那台适合时代的电视机。有一个可建造的2D马里奥，它沿着一个水平运行和跳跃，当你旋转一个手曲柄时，这个水平可以展开和滚动。
老实说，执行得很棒。只是真的，真的很聪明。它在经典游戏机四四方方的八位图形上播放，同时提供了一个与已经宣布的马里奥套装相对应的可爱的产品。在这里，协同的力量是强大的。一旦你移除一件作品，另一套中的马里奥实际上就会插到电视机的顶部，当他的2D对应者通过关卡时，它的扬声器会播放配乐和效果。
没有关于价格的消息，但我愿意打赌最终价格对许多粉丝来说都不是问题。此外，了解到我们对乐高的了解，我们似乎可以放心地猜测它不会便宜。套装共有2646件。这是我见过的技术上最令人印象深刻的乐高套装之一，从控制台的细节精确度开始，真正与推进背景的曲柄系统一起更上一层楼，因为马里奥连接在一根棍子上，上下弹跳。与此同时，另一组的马里奥似乎会扫描顶部一系列积木上的颜色，输出相应的声音。
老实说，这件事有效地同时触发了所有的人类怀旧中心，你无力否认，所以你不妨拨出一些现金，留出一些货架空间。如果让我猜的话，我会把它放在大概300美元的某个地方。考虑一下马里奥为孩子们设置了一个可玩的游戏，而这一个是为他们的父母提供的免费对话片段，他们从小就玩着原来的游戏系统。从8月份开始，它可以通过乐高网站(以及那些碰巧开门的实体店)和超级马里奥套装一起购买。它将在明年的某个时候在更多的零售商那里出售。
note_en:
Feel that? That’s the unmistakable and overwhelming sensation of nostalgia for your misspent youth coursing through your blood, as the 35th anniversary of the Nintendo Entertainment System is sneaking up on you. The original NES turns 35 years old tomorrow, and to celebrate another shocking reminder of our own mortality, Lego just announced another fantastic-looking set scheduled to arrive later this summer.
  After a brief tease, the NES Building Kit is now officially official, featuring a buildable console, cartridge and controller (even the RCA ports are present on the system’s side). The game can be loaded into the system and locked in place, and the controller has its own cable that plugs directly into the front. The pièce de résistance, however, is that period-appropriate television set. There’s a buildable 2D Mario that runs and jumps along a level that unfolds and scrolls as you spin a hand crank.
  Honestly, the execution is great. Just really, really clever. It plays on the boxy eight-bit graphics of the classic console, while offering an adorable counterpart to the  already announced Mario set. The power of synergy is strong here. The Mario from the other set actually plugs into the top of the TV set once you remove a piece, with its speaker playing out the soundtrack and effects as his 2D counterpart makes his way through the level.
  No word on pricing, but I’m willing to bet that the final price is no object for many fans. Also, knowing what we know about Lego, it seems safe to guess it’s not going to be cheap. There are 2,646 pieces in the set. It’s one of the most technically impressive Lego sets I’ve seen, starting with the detailed accuracy of the console and really taking it up a notch with the crank system that advances the background, as Mario, attached to a stick, bounds up and down. The Mario from the other set, meanwhile, appears to scan for the colors on the series of blocks at the top, outputting the corresponding sounds.
 Honestly, this thing effectively triggers all of the human nostalgia centers simultaneously, and you’re powerless to deny it, so you might as well earmark some cash and set aside some shelf space. If I had to guess, I would put it somewhere in the ballpark of $300. Consider the Mario set the playable one for the kids and this one a complimentary conversation piece for their parents who grew up playing the original system. It’s available through the Lego site (and those brick and mortar stores that happen to be open) starting August , along with the Super Mario set). It will be available at additional retailers at some point next year.
title: 在乐高新的2646件NES套件上手动操控超级马里奥兄弟的级别
title_en: Hand-crank a level of Super Mario Bros. on Lego’s new 2,646-piece NES kit

使用统一界面，开发人员可以在本地分析这些数据，而不必在平台之间移动数据集。
谷歌云数据分析工程副总裁兼总经理德班扬·萨哈(Debanjan Saha)在今天宣布这一消息之前的新闻发布会上表示：“我们的客户在BigQuery中存储了数PB的信息，他们知道这些信息是安全的，受到了保护。”“我们的许多客户在BigQuery中进行许多不同类型的分析。例如，它们使用内置的机器学习功能来运行实时分析和预测性分析。[…]。我们的许多客户对在GCP中使用BigQuery感到非常兴奋，他们也在问，‘他们如何才能将BigQuery的使用扩展到其他云？’“。
谷歌长期以来一直表示，它相信多云是未来的趋势-大多数竞争对手可能会同意这一点，尽管他们显然都希望你使用他们的工具，即使数据位于其他云中，或者是在平台外生成的。毕竟，正是这些工具和服务帮助企业利用所有这些数据，不同的供应商可以在这些数据中脱颖而出。考虑到谷歌云在数据分析方面的专业知识，BigQuery现在加入了多云的竞争，这或许并不令人惊讶。
萨哈说：“有了BigQuery Omni，客户就能得到他们想要的东西。”“他们想要分析他们的数据，无论这些数据位于何处，他们现在都可以通过BigQuery Omni获得这些数据。”
他指出，谷歌云相信这将帮助企业打破数据孤岛，获得对数据的新洞察力，同时允许开发人员和分析师使用标准的SQL界面。
今天的声明也是一个很好的例子，说明谷歌对Anthos的押注是如何获得回报的，因为它让公司不仅可以更容易地让客户管理他们的多云部署，而且还可以将自己的产品扩展到云中。这也解释了为什么Azure还不能使用BigQuery Omni，因为Anthos for Azure还在预览版中，而AWS支持在4月份就已经全面推出了。
note_en:
Using a unified interface, developers can analyze this data locally without having to move data sets between platforms.
 “Our customers store petabytes of information in BigQuery, with the knowledge that it is safe and that it’s protected,” said Debanjan Saha, the GM and VP of Engineering for Data Analytics at Google Cloud, in a press conference ahead of today’s announcement. “A lot of our customers do many different types of analytics in BigQuery. For example, they use the built-in machine learning capabilities to run real-time analytics and predictive analytics. […] A lot of our customers who are very excited about using BigQuery in GCP are also asking, ‘how can they extend the use of BigQuery to other clouds?’ ”
   Google has long said that it believes that multi-cloud is the future — something that most of its competitors would probably agree with, though they all would obviously like you to use their tools, even if the data sits in other clouds or is generated off-platform. It’s the tools and services that help businesses to make use of all of this data, after all, where the different vendors can differentiate themselves from each other. Maybe it’s no surprise then, given Google Cloud’s expertise in data analytics, that BigQuery is now joining the multi-cloud fray.
 “With BigQuery  Omni   customers get what they wanted,” Saha said. “They wanted to analyze their data no matter where the data sits and they get it today with BigQuery Omni.”
  He noted that Google Cloud believes that this will help enterprises break down their data silos and gain new insights into their data, all while allowing developers and analysts to use a standard SQL interface.
 Today’s announcement is also a good example of how Google’s bet on  Anthos is paying off by making it easier for the company to not just allow its customers to manage their multi-cloud deployments but also to extend the reach of its own products across clouds. This also explains why BigQuery Omni isn’t available for Azure yet, given that Anthos for Azure is still in preview, while AWS support became  generally available in April.
title: Google Cloud的新BigQuery Omni将允许开发者在GCP、AWS和Azure中查询数据
title_en: Google Cloud’s new BigQuery Omni will let developers query data in GCP, AWS and Azure

note_en:
The company announced today that it has raised $2.58 million in seed funding led by Founders Fund, with participation from Icehouse Ventures.
 The company said its website software, Narrative Publish, is currently used by tens of thousands of photographers. Part of the new funding will be used to market Narrative Select, which is available on a subscription basis, and build its user base as photographers around the world seek to recover from the the impact of COVID-19.
  Narrative is also recruiting tech talent from the United States and United Kingdom to work at its offices in New Zealand, which is  gaining more interest as an immigration destination because of its success in containing the spread of COVID-19.
 Narrative Select uses machine learning to flag photos as desirable or undesirable, focusing in particular on people in the photo. For example, if a subject is blurry or their eyes are closed, the photo is flagged as undesirable. The company says Narrative typically tags 30% of photos in a batch as undesirable, making the editing process faster for photographers. Narrative Select will be expanded to cover fashion, commercial and lifestyle photography, too.
title: 总部位于奥克兰的照片软件初创公司Narrative筹集了258万美元，由Founders Fund牵头
title_en: Auckland-based photo software startup Narrative raises $2.58 million led by Founders Fund

随着新型冠状病毒将人们放逐在家工作和娱乐，那些来回传输我们数据的“管道”对我们的生计变得越来越重要。然而，尽管我们作为消费者经常认为互联网是我们从Spectrum或TechCrunch的母公司Verizon等服务提供商那里购买的产品，但现实是，企业需要DNS和IP地址管理等关键网络服务，以优化其性能和成本。
这就是总部位于纽约市的NS1做得特别好的地方。两年前，我的同事罗恩·米勒(Ron Miller)首次为我们报道了该公司及其创始故事，这是我们深入了解纽约市企业软件生态系统的一部分。快进两年后，NS1的表现再好不过了：根据该公司的数据，仅在今年第一季度，新客户预订量同比增长了159%，目前为600名客户提供服务。
在市场的一个关键基础设施领域，这种牵引力吸引了更多增长资本的注意。今天，该公司宣布，Energy Impact Partners将牵头向这家初创公司进行4000万美元的D系列融资，使其迄今的融资总额达到1.25亿美元。Energy Impact Partners传统上投资于可持续能源初创公司，但最近已扩展到软件和互联网服务领域。这轮融资由EIR合伙人肖恩·切里安(Shawn Cherian)领导，他在6月初刚刚加入公司(与上任第一天达成交易完全不同)。
NS1联合创始人兼首席执行官克里斯·比弗斯表示，在过去的几个月里，新冠肺炎对这家初创公司的增长产生了巨大的影响。“例如，我们的一个大型软件客户(说)，我们冠状病毒工作组的第二大关键指标是由NS1管理的网络性能和饱和度。”由于延迟和可靠性的降低会极大地限制服务对居家工作人员和消费者的生存能力，客户已将网络管理放在了更高的优先位置。
“我最近几次用到的俏皮话是，数字化转型倡议已经从五年或十年压缩到现在的几个月或一年。每个人都必须加速所有这些事情，“比弗斯说。
该公司在DNS和IP管理等关键工具上加倍投入，但也根据客户的反馈推出了新功能。他说：“例如，我们推出了VPN转向功能，以帮助我们的客户优化他们的VPN足迹，因为很明显，这些突然变得比以往任何时候都更重要。”虚拟专用网络(VPN)允许员工登录到他们公司的网络，就像他们实际在办公室一样。
虽然NS1在银行有资金，客户的胃口也越来越大，但该公司也在全球大流行期间开始筹资。比弗斯说，一开始很难获得动力。“四月是个死区，”他说。“所有的风投都有点神志不清。”
5月初，随着风投们掌握了自己的投资组合，并开始根据新冠肺炎早期的经验教训，调查市场上的机会在哪里，潮流开始转向。他表示：“实际上，我们在5月初就开始获得大量的外来投资兴趣。”
比弗斯解释说：“可以称之为真正的冠状病毒募捐。”这是“端到端，就像不到一个月的时间来了解(切里安)到条款说明书，而且都是虚拟的。合伙人会议都是虚拟的，勤奋都是虚拟的。在整个筹款过程中，没有一次面对面的互动，其他所有参与这轮融资的人也是如此，所以所有最终没有写下获奖条款的人都是如此。
切里安脱颖而出的是Energy Impact Partners的投资组合，涉及能源、工业和物联网-这些行业正日益数字化，需要NS1提供的那种互联网基础设施服务。此外，切里安领导了一轮进入Packet的融资，这是纽约市的一家企业公司，以超过3亿美元的价格卖给了Equinox。Packet的创始人扎克·史密斯(Zac Smith)和比弗斯(Beevers)曾在Voxel共事，他们是曼哈顿基础设施工程师所谓的“Voxel黑手党”的一员。
有了新的资金，NS1打算继续扩大其在网络层的吸引力，同时在物联网等新市场上加倍投入。
note_en:
With the novel coronavirus marooning people at home for work and play, those “tubes” carrying our data back and forth have become ever more important to our livelihoods. Yet while we often as consumers think of the internet as what we buy from a service provider like Spectrum or TechCrunch’s parent company Verizon, the reality is that businesses need key network services like DNS and IP Address Management in order to optimize their performance and costs.
 That’s where New York City-based  NS1 has done particularly well. My colleague Ron Miller first  covered the company and its founding story for us two years ago, as part of  our in-depth look at the New York City enterprise software ecosystem. Fast forward two years, and NS1 couldn’t be doing better: in just the first quarter of this year, new customer bookings were up 159% year over year according to the company, and it currently serves 600 customers.
   That traction in a critical infrastructure segment of the market attracted the attention of even more growth capital. Today, the company announced that  Energy Impact Partners,   which has traditionally invested in sustainable energy startups but has recently expanded into software and internet services, is leading a $40 million Series D round into the startup, bringing its total fundraising to date to $125 million. The round was led by Shawn Cherian, a partner at EIR who  just joined the firm at the beginning of June (nothing like getting a deal done your first day on the job).
 Kris Beevers, cofounder and CEO of  NS1,   said that COVID-19 has had a huge impact on the startup’s growth the past few months. “For example, [a] large software customer of ours [said] that our number two KPI for our coronavirus task force is network performance and saturation as managed by NS1.” Customers have made network management significantly higher priority since degradations in latency and reliability can dramatically limit a service’s viability for stay-at-home workers and consumers.
  “The quip that I have used a few times recently is digital transformation initiatives have compressed from five or ten years down to months or a year at this point. Everybody’s just having to accelerate all of these things,” Beevers said.
 The company has doubled down on its key tools like DNS and IP management, but it has also launched new features using feedback from customers. “For example, we launched a VPN steering capability to help our customers optimize their VPN footprints because obviously those suddenly are more important than they’ve ever been,” he said. Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) allow employees to login to their company’s network as if they were physically present in the office.
 While NS1 had money in the bank and increasing appetite from customers, the company was also starting a fundraise in the middle of a global pandemic. Beevers said that it was hard at first to get momentum. “April was a dead zone,” he said. “All the VCs were sort of turtle up.”
 The tide began to turn by early May as VCs got a handle on their portfolios and started to survey where the opportunities were in the market given the lessons of the early days of COVID-19. “We actually started to get a huge amount of inbound interest in early May timeframe,” he said.
 “Call it like a true coronavirus fundraise,” Beevers explained. It was “end to end like less than a month getting to know [Cherian] to term sheet, and all virtual. Partner meeting was all virtual, diligence all virtual. Not a single in-person interaction in the whole fundraising process, and that was the case with everybody else who was involved in the round too, so all the folks that didn’t in the end write the winning term sheet.”
 What made Cherian stand out was Energy Impact Partners’ portfolio, which touches on energy, industry and IoT — sectors that are increasingly being digitized and need the kind of internet infrastructure services that NS1 provides. Also, Cherian led a round into  Packet,   which is  a fellow NYC enterprise company that  sold to Equinox for more than $300 million. Packet’s founder Zac Smith and Beevers worked together at Voxel and are part of the so-called “Voxel mafia” of infrastructure engineers in Manhattan.
  With the new funding, NS1 intends to continue to expand its traction in the network layer while also doubling down on new markets like IoT.
title: 在客户需求激增的情况下，NS1净赚4000万美元“真正的冠状病毒筹款”
title_en: NS1 nets $40M “true coronavirus fundraise” amidst surging customer demand

note:
“我们已经采用了各种隔离和沙盒技术作为我们云基础设施的一部分，以帮助确保我们的多租户架构的安全，”该公司在今天的声明中指出。“保密虚拟机通过提供内存加密将这一点提升到一个新的水平，以便您可以进一步隔离云中的工作负载。保密的虚拟机可以帮助我们所有的客户保护敏感数据，但我们认为这对那些受监管行业的人来说尤其有趣。“。
在后端，保密虚拟机利用AMD的安全加密虚拟化功能，该功能在其第二代EPYC CPU中可用。这样，数据在使用时将保持加密，实现这一点的加密密钥是在硬件中自动生成的，不能导出-因此，即使是谷歌也无法访问这些密钥。
想要将现有虚拟机迁移到机密虚拟机的开发人员只需点击几下即可完成。谷歌指出，它在其屏蔽的虚拟机之上建立了机密虚拟机，这些虚拟机已经提供了针对rootkit和其他攻击的保护。
AMD数据中心生态系统公司副总裁拉古·南比亚尔说：“借助内置的安全加密虚拟化，第二代AMD EPYC处理器提供了一种创新的基于硬件的安全功能，有助于保护虚拟化环境中的数据。对于N2D系列中的新Google Compute Engine Confidential VM，我们与Google合作，帮助客户保护数据并实现工作负载的性能。“。
最后一部分显然很重要，因为额外的加密和解密步骤至少会导致较小的性能损失。谷歌表示，它与AMD合作，并开发了新的开源驱动程序，以确保“机密虚拟机的性能指标与非机密虚拟机的性能指标接近。”至少根据谷歌自己到目前为止披露的基准，普通虚拟机和机密虚拟机的启动时间、内存读取和吞吐量性能几乎是相同的。
note_en:
“ We already employ a variety of isolation and sandboxing techniques as part of our cloud infrastructure to help make our multi-tenant architecture secure,” the company notes in today’s announcement. “Confidential VMs take this to the next level by offering memory encryption so that you can further isolate your workloads in the cloud. Confidential VMs can help all our customers protect sensitive data, but we think it will be especially interesting to those in regulated industries.”
 In the backend, Confidential VMs make use of AMD’s Secure Encrypted Virtualization feature, available in its second-generation EPYC CPUs. With that, the data will stay encrypted when used and the encryption keys to make this happen are automatically generated in hardware and can’t be exported — and with that, even Google doesn’t have access to the keys either.
   Developers who want to shift their existing VMs to a Confidential VM can do so with just a few clicks. Google notes that it built Confidential VMs on top of its  Shielded VMs, which already provide protection against rootkits and other exploits.
 “With built-in secure encrypted virtualization, 2 nd  Gen AMD EPYC processors provide an innovative hardware-based security feature that helps secure data in a virtualized environment,” said Raghu Nambiar, corporate vice president, Data Center Ecosystem, AMD. “For the new Google Compute Engine Confidential VMs in the N2D series, we worked with Google to help customers both secure their data and achieve performance of their workloads.”
 That last part is obviously important, given that the extra encryption and decryption steps do incur at least a minor performance penalty. Google says it worked with AMD and developed new open-source drivers to ensure that “ the   performance metrics  of Confidential VMs are close to those of non-confidential VMs.” At least according to the benchmarks Google itself has disclosed so far, both startup times and memory read and throughput performance are virtually the same for regular VMs and Confidential VMs.
title: 谷歌云推出保密虚拟机

这涉及到将开发人员和营销人员聚集在新的Acquia Open DXP平台的保护伞下。Acquia负责产品营销的高级副总裁Kevin Cochrane说：“这种方法有两个主要组成部分：”我们一直致力于在我们的套件中深度集成，并将我们新的基础性Drupal Cloud产品和我们新的基础性营销云产品整合在一起。
这些产品汇集了该公司在过去一年中进行的一系列收购，包括2019年5月针对营销自动化的Mautic，9月份针对低码开发的Cohesion，以及12月针对客户数据平台(CDP)的AgileOne。
Cochrane说，该公司正在利用这些收购以及他们内部开发的工具和即将发布的Drupal9为客户提供一种平台方法，在这种平台上，他们可以在Drupal云端构建内容，在营销云端利用客户数据。
在Drupal Cloud上，该公司提供了一套工具，其中包括集成开发环境(IDE)，开发人员可以在其中构建服务，而营销人员可以获得较低的代码供应，他们可以在其中拖放来自内部来源或开放源码社区的产品库中的内容和设计组件。它还包括其他组件，如安全和内容管理。
营销云是公司收集和管理客户数据的数据层，目的是在数字环境中提供更个性化、更有意义的体验。
近年来，营销自动化工具发生了变化，其目标是使用可用的海量数据为客户提供独特而有意义的体验，以构建更完整的客户图景，并在客户需要时在数字环境中提供他们需要的东西。这包括构建数字体验平台(DXP)和客户数据平台(CDP)。
通过将这些不同的元素结合在一起，Acquia试图让自己处于与该领域的大公司直接竞争的地位，比如Adobe和Salesforce提供了类似的统一方法。
Vista Equity Partners去年9月以10亿美元收购了Acquia。当时，公司创始人Dries Buytaert表示，成为Vista的一部分的优势之一是获得了在这一领域与更大公司竞争的资源，今天的声明可以从这个角度来看待。
note_en:
This involves bringing together developers and marketers under the umbrella of the new  Acquia   Open DXP platform. This approach has two main components: “What we’ve been working on is deep integration across our suite and pulling together our new foundational Drupal Cloud offering, and our new foundational Marketing Cloud offering,” Kevin Cochrane, senior vice president of product marketing at Acquia said.
 The offerings bring together a set of acquisitions the company made over the last year including  Mautic for marketing automation in May 2019,  Cohesion for low-code developing in September and  AgileOne in December for a customer data platform (CDP).
  Cochrane says that the company is leveraging these acquisitions along with tools they developed internally and the upcoming release of Drupal 9 to offer a platform approach for customers where they can build content on the Drupal Cloud side and leverage customer data on the Marketing Cloud side.
 On the Drupal Cloud, the company is offering a set of tools that includes an integrated development environment (IDE) where developers can build services, while marketers get a low code offering, where they can drag and drop content and design components from a library of offerings that could come from internal sources or the open source community. It also includes other components like security and content management.
 The Marketing Cloud is the data layer where companies collect and manage data about customers with the goal of offering a more personalized and meaningful experience in a digital context.
 Marketing automation tooling has shifted in recent years with the goal of providing customers with a unique and meaningful experience using the vast amount of data available to build a more complete picture of the customer and give them what they need, when they need it in a digital context. This has involved building a digital experience platform (DXP) and a customer data platform (CDP).
 By pulling together these different elements, Acquia is attempting to put itself in a position to compete directly with big players in this space like Adobe and Salesforce offering a similar unified approach.
 Vista Equity Partners    bought Acquia last September for $1 billion. At the time, company founder Dries Buytaert said one of the advantages of being part of Vista was to get the resources to compete with larger companies in this space, and today’s announcement could be seen in that light.
title: 新的Acquia平台希望将开发人员、营销人员和数据聚集在一起
title_en: New Acquia  platform looks to bring together developers, marketers and data

开发自动化产品和服务定价优化软件的Pricefx公司今天宣布，它筹集了6500万美元。一位发言人表示，新资金将用于扩大Pricefx的全球影响力，加快产品开发，扩大其合作伙伴生态系统，并评估收购，如2019年5月收购人工智能定价初创公司Brennus Analytics。
正确的定价对企业的成功至关重要。根据麦肯锡公司(McKinsey&Amp；Company)的一份报告，价格每上涨1%，平均营业利润就会增加8.7%，但估计有30%的定价决策未能带来最好的价格。与流行病相关的发展增加了对自动化解决方案的需求，由于即使在经济低迷时期也能获得强劲的投资回报，人们对定价软件的兴趣激增。
像Pricefx这样的公司，由埃森哲(Accenture)前顾问马丁·瑞克博士、Ariba资深人士马尔辛·西雄和程序员克里斯蒂安·特拉茨于2011年共同创立，为历史定价方法提供了一种动态的替代方案。这家总部位于慕尼黑的初创公司(在捷克共和国、瑞士和澳大利亚设有办事处)提供了一种软件即服务的价格优化解决方案，利用人工智能来摸索企业对企业和企业对消费者的产品定价，这种方法显然给继续砸钱的投资者留下了深刻印象。
Pricefx的云托管、模块化平台连接到应用程序和算法库，以分析和管理定价。该公司的PriceAnalyzer工具提供可视化和报告，突出表现不佳的产品、客户和营销计划，而另一个单独的组件-PriceOptimizer-利用机器学习和数据科学技术来测试各种定价策略、细分市场、销量和利润率。另一个工具PromotionManager允许客户定义、创建和管理折扣和定价优惠，以补充QuoteConfigurator服务，该服务将优化的报价反馈给客户关系管理系统。
Pricefx的RebateManager允许公司从单一仪表盘管理返点流程，其PFX平台产品将其基于SaaS的定价解决方案连接到现有的电子商务和企业资源管理系统。ChannelManager提供专为与当前技术堆栈无缝协作而设计的船舶和索赔管理工作流。而在过去几个月推出的Lightning，提供了一套标准的定价分析和配置价格报价(PCQ)管理工具，能够在6周或更短的时间内上线。
Pricefx去年扩大了在芝加哥的足迹，雇佣了40名员工，该公司声称，自今年3月以来，它签约了十几个新客户，年经常性收入超过1500万欧元(约合1690万美元)。该公司在37个国家和地区拥有100多家客户，其中包括米其林、施耐德电气、斯宾纳集团，以及一些世界上最大的制造、零售和能源企业。
Gartner的分析师预计，未来三年价格优化和管理市场将迅速增长，竞争比比皆是。其中有CoreXM的制造商Qualtrics；号称可以将盈利能力提高9%的BlackCurve；以及Prisync、netRivales和Competera。但联合创始人兼首席执行官西雄表示，Pricefx的产品多样性使其有别于其他公司。今年晚些时候，该公司计划推出其分析软件的自助版本，以及行业特定的加速器软件包、增强的CPQ功能和人工智能增强版本的PriceOptimizer。
Pricefx的C系列融资由Apax Partners的Apax Digital牵头，现有投资者Digital+Partners参与其中。这使得这家初创公司在2019年11月进行了2530万美元的B轮融资，融资总额达到1.3亿美元，是2018年估值的三倍。作为本轮融资的一部分，Apax Digital的马克·贝斯(Mark Beith)和丹尼尔·奥基夫(Daniel O‘Keefe)将加入Pricefx的监事会。
Cichon通过电子邮件告诉VentureBeat：“在过去的六个月里，我们看到定价对全球各地的企业变得越来越重要，我们再次证明，我们快速、灵活和友好的基于云的定价解决方案提供了一条增长之路，即使在困难时期也是如此。”这项最新的投资将使这一愿景继续向前发展，因为我们将继续为更多的客户创新，发展我们的合作伙伴生态系统，将业内最优秀的人才添加到我们杰出的团队中，并投资于更多的战略性收购。“。
Pricefx目前有370名全职员工，并表示将在未来12个月内寻求增加100多个新职位。
note_en:
Pricefx, a company developing automated product and service pricing optimization software, today announced that it raised $65 million. A spokesperson said the new funding will be used to expand Pricefx’s global presence, accelerate product development, extend its partner ecosystem, and evaluate acquisitions like its purchase of AI pricing startup Brennus Analytics in May 2019.
 Getting pricing right is critically important to business success. According to a McKinsey & Company  report, a 1% price increase translates to an 8.7% increase in operating profits on average, yet an estimated 30% of pricing decisions fail to deliver the best prices. Pandemic-related developments have increased the demand for  automated solutions, with pricing software seeing a surge in interest owing to the strong return on investment even in downturns.
  Firms like Pricefx, which was cofounded in 2011 by former Accenture consultant Dr. Martin Wricke, Ariba veteran Marcin Cichon, and programmer Christian Tratz, offer a dynamic alternative to “tried and tested” historical pricing methods. The Munich-based startup (with offices in the Czech Republic, Switzerland, and Australia) provides a software-as-a-service solution for price optimization that taps AI to suss out business-to-business and business-to-consumer product pricing, an approach that’s evidently impressed the investors who continue to throw money its way.
 Pricefx’s cloud-hosted, modularized platform connects to apps and algorithmic libraries to analyze and manage pricing. The company’s PriceAnalyzer tool delivers visualizations and reports that spotlight underperforming products, accounts, and marketing programs, while a separate component — PriceOptimizer — leverages machine learning and data science techniques to test various pricing strategies, segments, volumes, and margins. Another tool, PromotionManager, allows customers to define, create, and manage discounts and pricing offers, complementing the QuoteConfigurator service that feeds optimized quotes to customer relationship management systems.
  Pricefx’s RebateManager lets companies manage rebate processes from a single dashboard, and its PFX Platform product connects its SaaS-based pricing solutions to existing ecommerce and enterprise resource management systems. ChannelManager offers a ship and claims management workflow designed to work seamlessly with current tech stacks. And Lightning, which launched in the past few months, provides a suite of standard pricing analytics and configure-price-quote (PCQ) management tools able to go live in six weeks or less.
 Pricefx, which last year expanded its footprint in Chicago and hired on 40 employees, claims it signed on more than a dozen new customers since March of this year and hit over €15 million (~$16.9 million) in annual recurring revenue. The company’s over 100 clients in 37 countries include Michelin, Schneider Electric, Spinner Group, and some of the largest manufacturing, retailing, and energy businesses in the world.
 Analysts at Gartner  anticipate the price optimization and management market will grow rapidly in the next three years, and competition abounds. There’s Qualtrics, maker of CoreXM; BlackCurve, which claims to boost profitability by 9%; and Prisync, netRivals, and Competera. But cofounder and CEO Cichon says Pricefx’s product diversity sets it apart from the rest. Later this year, the company plans to introduce a self-service version of its analytics software as well as industry-specific Accelerator packages, enhanced CPQ functionality, and an AI-enhanced version of PriceOptimizer.
 Pricefx’s series C round was led by Apax Partners’ Apax Digital with participation from existing investor Digital+ Partners. It brings the startup’s total raised to date to $130 million following an  extended $25.3 million series B round in November 2019 at triple its valuation in 2018. As a part of this round, Apax Digital’s Mark Beith and Daniel O’Keefe will join Pricefx’s supervisory board.
 “Over the last six months, we’ve seen pricing become increasingly important to businesses across the globe, and we’ve proven again that our fast, flexible, and friendly cloud-based pricing solution provides a path towards growth, even when times get tough,” Cichon told VentureBeat via email. “This latest investment will keep that vision moving forward as we continue to innovate for more customers, grow our partner ecosystem, add the industry’s best talent to our exceptional team, and invest in more strategic acquisitions.”
 Pricefx has 370 full-time employees today and says it’ll look to add more than 100 new roles over the next 12 months.
title: Pricefx是一家开发自动化产品和服务定价优化软件的公司，在Apax Partners的Apax Digital的带领下，该公司筹集了6500万美元的C系列资金
title_en: Pricefx, which develops automated product and service pricing optimization software, raises $65M Series C led by Apax Partners' Apax Digital

伦敦--英国政府表示，将禁止电信公司购买中国华为技术有限公司(Huawei Technologies Co.)制造的新设备，并要求它们在2027年之前将华为的技术从其5G网络中移除，这一急剧转变标志着美国取得了重大胜利。
这一决定是在美国对华为电脑芯片的销售实施新的限制之后做出的，与此同时，英国和中国的关系正在更广泛地恶化，最近的一次是因为中国对前英国殖民地香港实施了新的安全法。
英国负责数字问题的大臣奥利弗·道登(Oliver Dowden)周二对下议院表示，从今年年底开始，将禁止新购买华为5G设备，到2027年，这家中国公司的设备将不得不从英国网络中剥离。就在1月份，英国还表示可以降低华为设备在其网络中的风险。
英国正在就何时禁止从该国光纤网络购买华为套件展开磋商。这之后将有一个过渡期，预计不会超过两年。
此举正值美国向欧洲各国政府施加越来越大的压力，要求将华为排除在5G网络之外。由美国国家安全顾问罗伯特·奥布莱恩(Robert O‘Brien)率领的美国高级官员以及意大利、德国、法国和英国的高级官员本周将在巴黎举行会议。
特朗普政府在5月份加大了对华为的压力，利用出口规则禁止全球供应商销售使用美国工具生产的该公司芯片。英国官员表示，这引发了人们对未来华为套件质量的质疑。
美国官员长期以来一直表示，北京可能会指示华为通过5G网络进行破坏或间谍活动，5G网络承诺为自动驾驶汽车等未来技术提供超高速无线速度。华为和中国政府否认这些指控。
华为英国业务负责人维克多·张(Victor Zhang)在6月份表示，该公司有经过证实的记录，很高兴与英国政府就“他们可能有的任何担忧进行合作，并继续我们过去10年来的工作关系。”华为在英国获得了相当大的市场份额，这是因为电信提供商认为该公司的设备质量高、价格便宜。
自从英国首相鲍里斯·约翰逊(Boris Johnson)今年1月批准华为建设部分5G网络以来，英国与中国的关系迅速恶化。当时，政府官员表示，与华为相关的风险可以缓解，这家设备制造商只被允许在5G网络中拥有最多35%的市场份额，并被排除在其“核心”之外，该网络上的许多信息都是在“核心”进行处理的。
英国国家网络安全中心(National Cyber Security Centre)是英国政府通信总部(GCHQ)电子情报机构的一部分，今年5月，由于美国的出口禁令，英国国家网络安全中心(National Cyber Security Centre)启动了对华为的新审查。政策的迅速逆转体现在冠状病毒大流行期间。
这一决定预计将引发更广泛的讨论，即英国、美国和其他盟国如何摆脱对中国技术和生产的依赖。近几个月来，医院和护理人员对中国制造的医疗用品的依赖突显了这个问题。
加拿大仍然是五眼情报联盟(包括美国、英国、澳大利亚和新西兰)中唯一一个尚未决定华为设备是否可以用于国内5G网络的国家。
英国电信业高管表示，设定快速的最后期限将华为设备从他们的网络中拆除，将导致客户覆盖中断，花费数十亿英镑，并推迟5G的引入。
沃达丰集团(Vodafone Group PLC)和英国电信集团(BT Group PLC)的高管上周对议会一个委员会表示，移除华为设备需要五到七年的时间框架，以避免中断。但一群保守党议员一直在向政府施压，要求政府加快拆除设备的速度。
note_en:
LONDON—The British government said it would bar telecom companies from purchasing new equipment made by China’s Huawei Technologies Co. and gave them until 2027 to remove its technology from their 5G networks, a sharp about-face that marks a  significant victory for the U.S.
  The decision follows  new U.S. restrictions on the sale of Huawei computer chips and comes amid a broader deterioration of relations between the U.K. and China, most recently relating to China’s imposition of a new security law over the former British colony of Hong Kong.
  Oliver Dowden, the British minister in charge of digital issues, told the House of Commons on Tuesday that new purchases of Huawei 5G equipment would be barred from the end of this year and that the Chinese company’s gear would have to be stripped out of British networks by 2027. As recently as January, the U.K. said it could mitigate the risk of Huawei equipment in its networks.
  The U.K is launching a consultation on when to ban the purchase of Huawei kit from the country’s fiber optic network. This will be followed by a transition period that isn’t expected to exceed two years.
    The move comes as U.S. pressure builds on European governments to  shut Huawei out of their 5G networks. Senior U.S. officials, led by U.S. national security adviser, Robert O’Brien, and his counterparts from Italy, Germany, France and the U.K. are meeting in Paris this week.
  The Trump administration ratcheted up its pressure on Huawei in May, using  export rules to bar global suppliers from selling the company’s chips produced using U.S. tools. British officials said this raised questions about the quality of Huawei kit in the future.
  U.S. officials have long said Beijing could direct Huawei to sabotage or spy through 5G networks, which promise to provide superfast wireless speeds for coming technologies such as self-driving cars. Huawei and the Chinese government reject the charges.
  Victor Zhang, head of Huawei’s U.K. operations, said in June that the company had a proven record and was happy to work with the British government regarding “any concerns they may have and to continue the working relationship we have had for the last 10 years.” Huawei has secured a significant U.K. market share, resulting from telecom providers’ views of the company’s equipment as high-quality and inexpensive.
  British relations with China have rapidly soured since Prime Minister Boris Johnson gave Huawei the  green light to build part of its 5G network in January this year. At the time, government officials said the risks related to Huawei could be mitigated, with the equipment maker permitted to only have a maximum 35% market share of the 5G network and to be excluded from its “core,” where much of the information on the network is processed.
  The U.K.’s National Cyber Security Centre, part of the nation’s GCHQ electronic intelligence agency, launched a new review of Huawei triggered by the U.S. export bans in May. The swift policy reversal played out during the coronavirus pandemic.
  The decision is expected to fuel broader discussions about how the U.K., U.S. and other allies can  wean themselves off Chinese technology and production, an issue underscored in recent months by reliance on Chinese-made medical supplies for hospitals and caregivers.
  Canada remains the lone country in the Five Eyes intelligence alliance—which also includes the U.S., U.K. Australia and New Zealand—that has yet to decide whether Huawei equipment can be used in the domestic 5G network.
  British telecoms executives have said that imposing a rapid deadline to tear out Huawei gear from their networks would lead to coverage blackouts for customers, cost billions of pounds and delay the introduction of 5G.
  Senior executives from Vodafone Group PLC and BT Group PLC told a parliamentary committee last week that a five-to-seven-year time frame would be needed to remove Huawei equipment to avoid disruption. But a group of Conservative lawmakers has been pressing the government to remove the equipment at a faster pace.
title: 英国表示，将禁止电信公司从华为购买新设备，并要求它们在2027年之前将华为的技术从其5G网络中移除
title_en: UK says it will bar telecom companies from purchasing new equipment from Huawei and gives them until 2027 to remove its technology from their 5G networks

今天很无聊：我的电视机前面有一盏灯-一台55英寸的TCL Roku电视机，就是那些只想买一台便宜的大电视的人-我觉得很烦人。它看起来像是一盏灯，基本上是为了表演而存在的。我们的许多电子产品都有这些恼人的灯，特别是机顶盒，大多数时间除了坐在那里，没有什么更多的功能。是的，指示灯是有用的--一种快速知道你的笔记本电脑是否在充电或者你已经收到某种通知的方式--但当它们太对着你的脸时，它们可能会让人抓狂。最近，我在Twitter上的一位粉丝迈克尔·克拉科夫斯基(Michael Krakovski)就这些闪光灯、以及巨大的蜂鸣声和互联网接入提出了我认为是一个令人惊叹的建议：一家公司应该积极开发没有这些闪光灯的产品，并专注于此。(有点开玩笑；他确实建议了一台没有互联网接入的路由器。)。我们是怎么来到这里的，到了一个指示灯一直令人讨厌的地方？那我们怎么出去呢？今天的沉闷让人们对形势有了一些苛刻的(但有重点的)了解。-Ernie@Destdium。
这就像Netflix for Mac应用程序：如果你是那种喜欢尝试新程序的人，看看哪些程序能坚持下去，那就试试SetApp，一个Netflix风格的Mac程序“应用程序商店”。它很便宜-每月只需9.99美元-这对你的工作效率将是一个巨大的恩惠。快看啊！
那一年，在通用电气工作的研究员尼克·霍洛尼亚克首次发明了发光二极管，这是一种可以显示可见光的电子元件，具体地说，就是红色。(在此之前，可以产生红外光的LED就已经存在了，正好赶上遥控器的发明。)。霍罗尼亚克在通用电气的一份新闻稿中指出，他的发明是由制造激光器的愿望推动的。“如果他们能制造激光器，我就能制造出比他们任何人都好的激光器，因为我制造了这种红色可见的合金。我就能看到发生了什么。它们就卡在红外线里了。“。霍洛尼亚克的发明依赖于半导体材料砷化镓磷化物的使用。(一种相关的材料，砷化镓，有时被认为是电脑中硅的替代品。)。
四年多前，美国医学会(American Medical Association)感到有必要公开表达对许多高速公路上日益增长的趋势的担忧-用使用LED照明的新照明系统取代旧的照明系统。
该协会警告说：“高强度LED照明设计会发出大量的蓝光，肉眼看起来是白色的，在夜间产生比传统照明更糟糕的眩光。”强烈的蓝光LED照明带来的不适和残疾会降低视觉敏锐度和安全性，导致担忧并造成道路危险。
但是为什么特别是蓝光呢？它怎么会让你的眼睛这么难看呢？问题有没有缩小？
当然，这是一个科学家们几十年来一直在研究的问题…。却没有意识到这一点。1958年，研究人员J·伍德兰·黑斯廷斯(光生物学专家)和比阿特丽斯·M·斯威尼(植物生理学家)发表了一篇论文，题为《多色甲藻发光的昼夜节律特征》，分析了不同颜色的光对多色甲藻的影响，这是一种具有发光能力的甲藻(一种单细胞有机体)。
这篇早期的论文实际上比可见LED早了几年，它指出，有机体对不同颜色的光的反应不同，对蓝色的反应最强，对红色的反应可能较小。这影响了他们的昼夜节律，或者说他们的睡眠-觉醒周期。这是一项有趣的研究，但直到几十年后，两位科学家都去世后，它才在更广泛的科学界获得了重大意义。
在2010年发表在学术期刊“环境健康展望”上的一篇文章中，作者大卫·C·霍尔兹曼指出了其中的原因--事实证明，影响单细胞生物体的东西也会影响人类：
黑斯廷斯和斯威尼的论文发表在1958年12月的“生物公报”上，几十年来尘埃落定。没有人认为这些发现可能与人类有任何关联，当时人们普遍认为人类的昼夜节律对光相对不敏感。
但过去20年的科学发现改变了这一切。光不仅可以重置人类的昼夜节律，而且同样对甲藻有最强影响的蓝光也有同样的能量来重置我们自己的时钟-尽管大多数可见波长都可以重置时钟，但蓝色以最高的效率完成了这项工作。
蓝光是使光线可见和充满活力的关键部分，它无处不在，特别是在屏幕和其他人造光源中。人们普遍认为，如果你事先一直在看笔记本电脑或智能手机，这就是为什么你可能很难上床睡觉的原因。
当然，这不是什么新信息，如果你在过去十年里在互联网上读过任何科普内容，你肯定听说过蓝光是如何对你有害并损害你的睡眠周期的。
但这一解释切中了我们面对的问题的核心，这些LED全天候闪烁在我们的脸上。同样倾向于最亮的蓝色LED实际上是最难产生的。复杂到当三位日本研究人员--中村修二、赤崎勇和天野浩--弄清楚了这一点时，他们最终获得了诺贝尔物理学奖。
(旁注：小尼克·霍洛尼亚克Jr.。(值得注意的是，当蓝色LED的发明者获得诺贝尔奖时，他没有获得诺贝尔奖。)。
蓝色LED的发明是给我们带来白色LED光的核心，这给照明行业带来了革命性的变化-但一些人认为这很苛刻，这意味着它可能会有与蓝光相同的问题。(因此，美国医学会发出信息，反对将其用于路灯。)
但甚至在此之前，人们就已经注意到路由器和计算机显示器等电子产品中蓝色LED的严酷程度。正如伊恩·约翰逊在2004年为“环球邮报”所写的那样，在对蓝光的担忧真正成为主流之前：
蓝色LED具有刺眼的清晰度，可以立即吸引眼球，令人着迷。它们闪烁，它们闪烁，对于如此微小的光点来说，它们可以变得令人难以置信地强烈-它们真的很漂亮。
它们装饰在我的显示器、手持扫描仪、网络摄像头、USB集线器、蓝牙接入点、WiFi适配器、我的扬声器的桌面音量控制、外置硬盘驱动器、视频编辑外设(实际上是脉冲)和充电鼠标的扩展底座上。我的新电脑前面还有六个。
而且它们确实穿透了。例如，我的无线路由器使用蓝灯让我了解网络流量模式，尽管它们在厚厚的塑料层后面，但它们刺耳到让我分心的地步。然而，它们在某种程度上没有附近电视机上的白光那么让人分心，白光是由蓝色LED与其他颜色组合而成的，而且明显比需要的大得多。(好的一面是，我最近了解到，这盏烦人的灯并不是特别必要，可以关掉。我刚把它关掉了。感谢上帝。)。
但即使在一切变蓝之前，它也不像红色LED-许多闹钟和老式Hayes调制解调器的基础-或绿色LED更好。他们往往会分心，很大程度上是因为缺乏细微之处。他们让人知道他们的外表，即使他们的光芒天生就是聚焦的，紧紧缠绕在一起的。
凌晨2点。早上，一个小小的LED指示灯就足以把你从睡眠模式中拉出来，把你从正常的昼夜节律中拉出来。这可能会让人分心。
就在软件开发人员迈克尔·赫夫和洛娜·赫夫开发出F.lux应用程序的那一年，为了更好地匹配昼夜节律，该应用程序将蓝光从屏幕上移除。这款应用程序最初是一款Mac应用程序，但后来扩展到了所有主要的软件平台，有效地使屏幕变黄，使其在最低设置下带有蜡烛的色调。(它还允许您使用“暗室”模式，本质上使您的计算机看起来像是使用的是1980年的黑黄色CRT。这是一种整洁。)。当然，苹果在几年前就锁定了它，但正如Lifehacker指出的那样，F.lux的定制化程度要高得多。
绿色不像蓝色或红色那么糟糕，但它仍然吸引着你。(Michael Galpert/Flickr)
现在，指示灯明显早于LED。稍微挖掘一下历史，你会发现19世纪铁路和轮船使用指示灯或警示灯的例子。1867年出版的“美国海军信号代码”讨论了在晚间使用的各种“信号灯”。
早在1897年，火奴鲁鲁的一家歌剧院也使用了当时的新白炽灯，使它们成为这种形式的著名早期使用者。
与此同时，1927年的一篇“大众科学”(Popular Science)文章提出了在车辆内安装自己的指示灯的理由。
当然，汽车在依赖发光二极管之前很久就有了指示灯，虽然LED肯定有它们的用途-特别是在你需要在黑暗中看到灯光的地方，比如汽车仪表盘或背光电脑键盘-人们可以争辩说，当谈到指示灯LED时，我们可能已经到了过度使用的地步。
让我举一个例子。去年，我买了一副书架扬声器，是一家名为Edifier的公司制造的。我喜欢这些扬声器，因为它们让我可以毫无顾虑地表达杰夫·罗森斯托克的担忧。他们声音很大，而且听起来很好听。我买它们的第二个原因是因为它们支持蓝牙，当我想用没有耳机插孔的手机播放音乐时，蓝牙偶尔会派上用场。
但这样做有一种恼人之处。将扬声器设置为蓝牙模式意味着原本不亮的扬声器突然发出明亮的蓝光从侧面射出。我不需要那盏灯。我可以找出扬声器是不是用另一种方式连接的。可以说，对于扬声器来说，有一些其他方式来明确设置模式会更聪明，可能是通过开关或类似的东西(尽管这会使其远程控制能力变得复杂，所以可能不会)。
为了跟进这一点，我想知道是否有目前正在使用的LED灯的使用案例，在这些用例中，替代技术将同样有效，而不会分散注意力。例如，在路由器、电子墨水或电子墨水的情况下，对于基本上是拨号调制解调器时代遗留下来的灯阵列来说，它们将是非常合适的替代品。
甚至还有一些刚刚萌芽的彩色电子墨水还没有流行起来，本质上是因为它们有与LED相反的问题：与你的笔记本电脑显示器相比，它们是暗淡的，相比之下，响应时间较慢。看起来彩色电子墨水在经过多年的努力完善技术后，可能要到明年才能最终进入主流市场。
即便如此，它们对于用户通常只想得到某件事正在工作的通知的用例来说也是完美的。
同样，在手机之外的触觉反馈方面，可能还有更多的工作空间。振动的迹象在某些类型的电子产品上可能更有效。
但由于无处不在、成本(你在亚马逊上花不到7美元就能买到100包蓝色LED)，以及可能缺乏创造力的原因，我们拿到了这些LED灯，从来没有真正考虑过在某些情况下可能会有比在你脸上放一个指示灯更好的产品设计解决方案。
诚然，如果LED灯的设计将亮度降至最低，那么在某些情况下，LED灯实际上可能仍然有意义。比方说，光线更多地出现在底部的光晕中，对你的视网膜隐藏了更强烈的光线元素。通过软化效果，它可能会使它的问题变得更小。
毕竟，如果你使用的是电视机或显示器，什么时候开什么时候不开通常是显而易见的。
当然，有很多LED灯已经在你的房子里徘徊，可能需要稍微减少对视网膜的影响。
这是Dim it Sheets显然能够做到的一个例子。(通过Dim It网站)。
有些产品确实做到了这一点。其中一家最知名的公司名为Dim It，该公司销售的床单基本上掩盖了特别明亮的LED，以帮助抑制其一些更具破坏性的影响。该公司的网站讨论了这项技术，认为这是一种神奇的材料：
每套调光套装包括两张调光片。这些薄片可以很容易地裁剪和修剪成您需要的任何形状，以适应您的设备LED灯。过高的亮度会立即降低到更舒适的水平。此外，调光它的调光片可以分层，以进一步增加调光效果的需要。使用Dim的另一个好处是它可以通过静电粘附附着到表面。因此，没有凌乱的粘合剂，Dim它可以很容易地移除，重新定位，并根据需要多次重复使用！
我发现，是的，我可以关掉Roku电视屏幕底部恼人的LED，这让我感到很有勇气，我可能会把这作为一种潜在的解决方案，来抑制我生活中一些更具侵略性的蓝灯-我的显示器上的那个，我至强电源按钮上的那个，以及我路由器上的那些明亮的蓝灯。除了科学之外，这些灯没有理由需要如此强烈的亮度。
有这样的产品存在真是太好了。话又说回来，总是有遮蔽胶带的。
觉得这本书有趣吗？和朋友一起分享吧！再次感谢我们的赞助商SetApp！
note_en:
Today in Tedium: There’s a light on the front of my TV set— a 55-inch TCL Roku TV set, of the kind that people who just want an inexpensive big TV buy—that I find annoying. It seems like a light that exists basically for show. A lot of our electronics have these annoying lights, especially set-top boxes that don’t really do much more than sit there most of the time. Yes, indicator lights are useful—a quick way of knowing whether your laptop is charging or you’ve gotten a notification of some kind—but they can be maddening when they’re too in-your-face. Recently, a follower of mine on Twitter, Michael Krakovskiy,  posed what I thought was an amazing suggestion regarding these flashing lights, along with loud buzzing noises and internet access: A company should actively develop products without them, and specialize in it. (Somewhat in jest; he did suggest a router without internet access.) How did we get here, to a place where indicator lights became a constant annoyance? And how do we get out? Today’s Tedium puts some harsh (but focused) light on the situation.  — Ernie @ Tedium
 It’s like Netflix for Mac apps: If you’re the kind of person who likes trying out new programs to see what sticks,   try SetApp, a Netflix-style “app store” for Mac programs. It’s cheap—just $9.99 a month—and it’ll be a huge boon to your productivity.   Check it out!
 The year that Nick Holonyak, a researcher working for General Electric, first invented the light-emitting diode, an electric component that could display visible light—specifically, the color red. (LEDs that could produce infrared light were in existence before then, just in time for the invention of the remote control.)  Holonyak noted in a GE news release that his invention was driven by a desire to build a laser. “If they can make a laser, I can make a better laser than any of them because I’ve made this alloy that is in the red—visible. And I’m going to be able to see what’s going on. And they’re stuck in the infrared.” Holonyak’s invention relied on the use of gallium arsenide phosphide, a semiconductor material. (A related material, gallium arsenide,  has at times been pitched as replacement for silicon in computers.)
 A little over four years ago, the American Medical Association felt compelled to publicly raise concerns with a growing trend on many highways—the replacement of older lighting systems with new ones that used LED lighting.
  “High-intensity LED lighting designs emit a large amount of blue light that appears white to the naked eye and create worse nighttime glare than conventional lighting,”  the association warned. “Discomfort and disability from intense, blue-rich LED lighting can decrease visual acuity and safety, resulting in concerns and creating a road hazard.”
 But why blue light in particular? What about it makes it so awful on your eyes? And does the problem scale down?
 This is a question, of course, that scientists have been researching for decades … without realizing it. In 1958, researchers J. Woodland Hastings (an expert in photobiology) and Beatrice M. Sweeney (a plant physiologist) published a paper, “ Characteristics of the diurnal rhythm of luminescence in Gonyaulax polyedra,” that analyzed the impact of different colors of light on the Gonyaulax polyedra, a kind of dinoflagellate (a single-celled organism) with the ability to emit light.
 This early paper, which actually predates the visible LED by a few years, noted that the organism reacted differently to varying colors of light, with its strongest reaction to the color blue, and perhaps a lesser one to the color red. It affected their circadian rhythm, or their sleep-wake cycle. It was an interesting piece of research, but it didn’t gain major significance in the broader scientific community until decades later, after both scientists had passed.
 In a 2010 article for the academic journal  Environmental Health Perspectives, author David C. Holzman noted the reason for that—it turns out that what affects single-cell organisms also affects humans:
 Hastings and Sweeney’s paper, published in the December 1958  Biological Bulletin, gathered dust for decades. No one thought these findings might hold any relevance for humans, whose circadian rhythms were then widely believed to be relatively insensitive to light.
 But scientific discoveries in the past two decades have changed all that. Not only does light reset the human circadian rhythm, but the same blue light that has the strongest impact on dinoflagellates has equal power to reset our own clocks—although most visible wavelengths can reset the clock, the blues do the job with the greatest efficiency.
 And blue light, a key part of what makes light visible and vibrant, is everywhere, especially in screens and other artificial light sources. It’s widely considered the reason why you might struggle to go to bed if you’ve been looking at your laptop or smartphone beforehand.
 This, of course, is not new information and if you’ve read any pop-science content on the internet in the past decade, you’ve most assuredly heard about how blue light is bad for you and harms your sleep cycle.
   But this explanation gets to the heart of the problem we face with individual LEDs that flash in our faces at all hours of the day. Blue LEDs, which again tend to be the brightest, were actually the hardest to generate. The complication was such that, when three Japanese researchers—Shuji Nakamura, Isamu Akasaki, and Hiroshi Amano—did figure it out, it eventually  earned them a Nobel Prize for physics.
 (Side note: Nick Holonyak Jr. was notably salty  about not getting a Nobel Prize when the inventors of the blue LED did.)
 The invention of the blue LED is at the center of the what gives us white LED light, which has revolutionized the lighting industry—but which some consider harsh, which means it can have the same problems as blue light. (Hence the AMA messaging against using it for street lights.)
 But even before then, people were noticing the harshness of blue LEDs in electronics such as routers and computer monitors.  As Ian Johnson wrote for  The Globe and Mail in 2004, before the concerns about blue light really went mainstream:
 Blue LEDs have a piercing clarity that draws the eye immediately, and which can mesmerize. They shimmer, they twinkle, and they can be incredibly intense for such tiny points of light—they’re really quite beautiful.
  They adorn my monitor, handheld scanner, webcam, a USB hub, a Bluetooth access point, a WiFi adapter, a desktop volume control for my speakers, an external hard drive, a video editing peripheral (that one actually pulses), and the docking cradle for a rechargeable mouse. There are six more on the front of my new computer.
 And they do pierce. My wireless router, for example, uses blue lights to let me know about network traffic patterns, and they are harsh to the point of distraction, despite being behind a thick layer of plastic. Yet they’re somehow less distracting than the white light on the nearby TV set, which is produced by combining blue LEDs with other colors, and significantly larger than it needs to be. (On the plus side, I learned recently that this annoying light, which is not particularly necessary,  can be turned off. I just turned it off. Thank the Lord.)
 But even before everything went blue, it wasn’t like red LEDs—the basis of many  alarm clocks and  old Hayes modems—or green LEDs were any better. They tend to distract in large part because of a lack of subtlety. They make their appearance known, even as their glow is inherently focused and tightly wound.
 At 2 a.m. in the morning, a small LED indicator can be enough to thrust you from sleep mode, to pull you out of a normal circadian rhythm. And it can be quite distracting.
 The year that software developers Michael and Lorna Herf  came up with the application F.lux, which removes blue light from screens in an effort to better match circadian rhythms. The application, which started as a Mac app but has since expanded to every major software platform, effectively yellows out the screen, making it carry the tone of a candle at its lowest setting. (It also allows you to use a “dark room” mode that essentially makes your computer look like it’s using a black-and-yellow CRT from 1980. It’s kind of neat.) Apple, of course, Sherlocked it a few years ago, but as  Lifehacker notes,  F.lux remains far more customizable.
 Green isn’t as bad as blue or red, but it still grabs you. ( Michael Galpert/Flickr)
  Now, indicator lights obviously predate the LED. Doing a little digging through history, you’ll find examples of railroads and ships using indicator lights or warning lights during the 19th century.  The United States Naval Signal Code, published in 1867, discusses a wide array of “signal lights” to be used during the evening hours.
 A Honolulu opera house also used then-new incandescent indicator lights way back in 1897, making them a notable early user of the form.
   A 1927  Popular Science article, meanwhile,  makes the case for installing your own indicator lights inside a vehicle.
 Of course, cars had indicator lights long before they relied on light-emitting diodes, and while LEDs definitely have their purpose—especially, for example, in places where you need to see lights in the dark, such as a car dashboard or a backlit computer keyboard—one can argue that we might be at a point of overuse when it comes to indicator LEDs.
 Let me offer an example. Last year  I got a pair of bookshelf speakers made by a company named Edifier. I love these speakers because they allow me to crank up  Jeff Rosenstock’s  Worry without worry. They’re loud, and they sound good. A secondary reason I got them was because they support Bluetooth, which occasionally comes in handy during times I want to play music from my phone, which doesn’t have a headphone jack.
 But there’s an annoyance in doing so. Setting the speaker into Bluetooth mode means that the speakers, which are otherwise unlighted, suddenly have a bright blue light kicking out the side. I don’t need that light. I can figure out if the speaker is connected another way. It would arguably be smarter for the speaker to have some other way of making it clear the mode is set, possibly through a switch or something similar (though that would complicate its remote control capabilities, so maybe not).
 To follow through the point, I wonder if there are use cases where LED lights are currently in use where an alternative technology would be just as effective without the distractive downsides. For example, in the case of routers, electronic ink, or e-ink, would be a more than suitable replacement for the array of lights that are essentially a holdover from the days of dial-up modems.
 There are even some budding examples of  color e-ink that have yet to take off essentially because they have the opposite problem to LEDs: They’re dull compared to your laptop monitor, with slow response time in comparison. It looks like color e-ink  may only finally hit the mainstream market next year after years of work perfecting the technology.
 Even so, they’d be perfect for a use case where often, users just want to have a notification that something is working.
 Likewise, there is likely room for more to be done with haptic feedback outside of phones. A sign of vibration may be more effective with some kinds of electronics.
 But for reasons related to ubiquity, cost (you can literally buy  a 100-pack of blue LEDs for less than $7 on Amazon), and perhaps a lack of creativity, we got a hold of these LED lights and never really considered that there might be better product design solutions in some cases than having an indicator light in your face.
 Admittedly, LED lights may in fact still make sense in some cases if they’re designed in a way that minimizes their luminance. Say, for example, the light shows up in more of a halo at the bottom, hiding the more intense element of the light from your retinas. By softening the effect it could potentially make it less problematic.
 After all, if you’re using a TV set or a monitor, it’s generally obvious when it’s on and when it’s not.
 Of course, there are lots of LED lights already lingering throughout your house that likely need to create a little less retinal impact.
  An example of what Dim It sheets apparently are able to do. (via the Dim It website)
 And there are products that actually do this. One of the best known companies, a firm named Dim It,  sells sheets that essentially cover up particularly bright LEDs to help tamp down some of their more disruptive effects.  The company’s website discusses the technology as something of a miracle material:
 Each package of Dim It includes two light dimming sheets. These sheets can be easily cut and trimmed to be any shape you need to fit over your devices LED lights. Instantly, the excessive brightness is reduced to a more comfortable level. Plus, Dim It’s light dimming sheets can be layered to further increase the dimming effect as needed. An added benefit of using Dim It is that it adheres to surfaces via static cling. Thus, there’s no messy adhesive and Dim It can be easily removed, re-positioned, and reused as many times as needed!
 Feeling emboldened by my discovery that, yes, I can turn off the annoying LED on the bottom of my Roku TV’s screen, I might just look into this as a potential solution to tamp down some of the more aggressive blue lights in my life—the one on my monitor, the one on the power button of my Xeon, and those bright blue lights on my router. There is no reason these lights need to be as aggressively bright as they are, other than, y’know, science.
 It’s nice that a product like this exists. Then again, there’s always masking tape.

title: 我们是不是让LED指示灯走得太远了？
title_en: Have we let the LED indicator light go too far?

note:
周一，数百名海军水兵继续在圣地亚哥海军基地与邦霍姆·理查德号航空母舰(USS Bonhomme Richard)上的熊熊大火搏斗，这是有史以来在战斗之外吞没美国军舰的最严重的大火之一。
这场始于周日上午的大火摧毁了这艘船的前桅杆，并在其上层建筑下造成了海军官员所说的地狱，温度高达1000华氏度。
海军一位女发言人说，截至周一下午，所有59名伤者-36名水兵和23名平民-已从几家地区医院出院。他们已经接受了包括中暑和烟雾吸入在内的伤害治疗。
现在，人们的注意力转向了这艘船的命运，以及滚滚浓烟可能对海军基地附近社区造成的影响。
这场毁灭性的大火及其迅速蔓延到整艘船，是军舰积极防范的威胁。所有海军新兵都接受过一定程度的消防训练，他们被教导火灾的威胁甚至比敌人的鱼雷或导弹更大。
由于这种持续不断的担忧，像邦霍姆·理查德号这样的船只通常会保留大约五分之一或六分之一的船员，即使在他们的母港，也足以在紧急情况下开航，或者在需要时派遣消防人员。
周一，当直升机将巨大的水桶倾倒在飞行甲板上时，拖船向邦霍姆·理查德号的一个开放的机库海湾喷水。海军少将菲利普·E·索贝克(Philip E.Sobeck)在一次吹风会上说，船上的水手被迫同时给船“脱水”，以稳定邦霍姆·理查德号(Bonhomme Richard)，这艘船正从消防用水向一侧倾斜。
海军远征打击群指挥官索贝克上将(Admiral Sobeck)表示，这艘船的皮肤一直被烧毁。海军远征打击群的旗舰是邦霍姆·理查德号(Bonhomme Richard)。他没有直接回答有关船体是否受损的问题。
当被问及这艘船能否得救时，索贝克上将说：“现在，我们正竭尽全力做到这一点。一旦我们把火扑灭了，这是我们的首要任务，然后我们就会做出评估。“。他后来补充说，他“绝对希望”这艘船能再次起航。
乍一看，邦霍姆·理查德号看起来很像一艘较小版本的海军航空母舰：它从船头到船尾有一个800英尺长的飞行甲板，上面载有直升机和F-35喷气式飞机。但它的主要任务是作为一艘巨大的两栖攻击舰，能够搭载大约1200名海军陆战队士兵，并用两栖车辆将他们降落在海滩上，同时其飞机提供近距离空中支持。
Bonhomme Richard号于1998年投入使用，以本杰明·富兰克林(Benjamin Franklin)的笔名命名，是同级中的八艘船之一，在日本佐世保(Sasebo)的海军第七舰队部署了六年后，造船厂漫长的维修期即将结束。
起火的确切原因尚不清楚。但索贝克上将说，船上的水手说，它是从一个空旷的空地开始的，那里用来存放海军陆战队使用的卡车和两栖车辆，可能是由重型纸箱和其他储存材料提供的燃料。
索贝克上将说，这艘船的一种灭火方法，即哈龙系统，因为它正在船厂工作，所以没有运行。但由于哈龙通常只安装在某些离散的舱室，如机舱，目前尚不清楚这是否对灭火有影响。
“邦霍姆·理查德号”(Bonhomme Richard)的一柱烟柱顺着盛行的风向南席卷，推动一股携带少量油漆、沥青、塑料和火山灰的烟柱穿过加利福尼亚州的帝国海滩(Imperial Beach)，进入墨西哥。在相反的方向，居民们也报告说闻到了远至25英里以北的德尔马(Del Mar)火灾的烟味。
圣地亚哥县空气污染控制区周一警告称，由于细颗粒物水平上升，船上冒出的浓烟可能会带来健康风险。
“在浓烟地区，假设空气质量水平对敏感群体不健康，对所有个人都不健康，”该地区警告说。
火灾发生在一个周日，当时大多数船员都不在船上，这一事实突显了火灾对军舰构成的特殊而持久的威胁，即使在港口，军舰的工业工作和维护工作也可以在一天中的任何时间进行，即使是在周末。
像邦霍姆·理查德号(Bonhomme Richard)这样的船只经常在日本的巡航中轮换，那里的行动节奏是海军中最高的，这导致了严重的损耗，通常在它们返回美国后，会在干船坞里花很长时间来解决这个问题。在大火之前，邦霍姆·理查德号在下一次部署之前正在完成造船厂的延长工期。现在这艘船面临着不确定的未来。
海军甚至不能开始知道未来的工作范围，直到最后一场大火被扑灭，所有炽热的钢材都冷却了。周日下午，邦霍姆·理查德号被完全疏散后，身穿空气坦克的消防员已返回船内。
然而，即使在灾难性破坏的情况下，海军也有很长的让船只起死回生的历史。
1987年，史塔克号航空母舰在被两枚伊拉克导弹击中后获救，并重新服役。第二年，美国护卫舰塞缪尔·B·罗伯茨号(USS Samuel B.Roberts)在波斯湾袭击了一枚海军水雷后幸存下来。
1991年波斯湾战争期间，“普林斯顿”号巡洋舰和“的黎波里”号两栖舰艇都触雷，后来又重返岗位。
2000年，科尔号航空母舰在被一艘装满炸药的自杀船袭击后，在船体上撕开了一个30英尺宽的洞，从而保持了漂浮状态。它被放在一艘重型升降船上，在后来恢复服务之前，该船将其运回美国进行维修。
近年来，在海上相撞后，类似的升降船也被用来运载另外两艘驱逐舰。其中一艘名为“菲茨杰拉德”号(USS Fitzgerald)的船停泊在邦霍姆理查德号(Bonhomme Richard)的码头对面，在被更大的船冒出的滚滚浓烟淹没后，最终被拖船移动。
note_en:
Hundreds of Navy sailors were continuing on Monday to battle a raging fire on the U.S.S. Bonhomme Richard at a San Diego naval base, one of the worst blazes to ever engulf an American warship outside of combat.
 The fire, which  began Sunday morning, has destroyed the ship’s forward mast and created what Navy officials described as an inferno beneath its superstructure, reaching temperatures as high as 1,000 degrees.
 All 59 people who were injured — 36 sailors and 23 civilians — were released from several area hospitals by Monday afternoon, a Navy spokeswoman said. They had been treated for injuries including heat exhaustion and smoke inhalation.
 Now, attention has turned to the fate of the ship, and to the impact that the billowing smoke may have on communities near the naval base.
 The devastating fire, and its rapid spread throughout the ship, is a threat that warships zealously guard against. All Navy recruits receive some degree of firefighting training, and they are taught that a fire is a greater threat than even enemy torpedoes or missiles.
 Because of that constant concern, ships like the Bonhomme Richard usually keep about one-fifth or one-sixth of their crew aboard even while in their home port, enough to get underway in an emergency, or to field firefighting crews should the need arise.
 On Monday, tugboats shot water into an open hangar bay of the Bonhomme Richard as helicopters tipped huge buckets onto the flight deck. Sailors on board were forced to “dewater” the ship at the same time to stabilize the Bonhomme Richard, which is listing to one side from water used in firefighting, Rear Adm. Philip E. Sobeck  said at a briefing.
 Admiral Sobeck, the commander of the Navy expeditionary strike group whose flagship is the Bonhomme Richard, said there had been burn damage all the way through the skin of the ship. He did not directly respond to a question about whether the hull had been damaged.
 Asked whether the ship could be saved, Admiral Sobeck said: “Right now, we’re doing everything we can to do exactly just that. Once we get the fire out, which is the priority, then we make that assessment.” He added later that he was “absolutely hopeful” that the ship would sail again.
 At first glance, the Bonhomme Richard looks much like a smaller version of a Navy aircraft carrier: It has an 800-foot-long flight deck from bow to stern that carries helicopters and F-35 jets. But its primary mission is as a massive amphibious assault ship, capable of carrying about 1,200 Marines and landing them on a beach with amphibious vehicles while its aircraft simultaneously provide close-air support.
 Commissioned in 1998, the Bonhomme Richard, named for Benjamin Franklin  using his pen name, is one of eight ships in its class, and was nearing the end of a lengthy period of shipyard repairs following a six-year deployment with the Navy’s Seventh Fleet in Sasebo, Japan.
 The exact cause of the blaze was unclear. But Admiral Sobeck said sailors on board said that it had started in a cavernous open area used for storing trucks and amphibious vehicles used by Marines and that it may have been fueled by heavy-duty cardboard boxes and other materials in storage.
 Admiral Sobeck said one of the ship’s methods of fire suppression, its halon system, was not operating because it was being worked on in the shipyard. But since halon is typically installed only in certain discrete compartments, such as in engine rooms, it was not clear whether that had an impact in fighting the fire.
 A pillar of smoke from the Bonhomme Richard has swept south with the prevailing winds, driving a plume carrying bits of paint, asphalt, plastic and ash across Imperial Beach, Calif., and into Mexico. In the opposite direction, residents have also reported smelling smoke from the fire as far away as Del Mar, 25 miles north.
 The San Diego County Air Pollution Control District  warned on Monday that the thick smoke swelling from the ship could pose health risks because of rising levels of fine particulates.
 “In areas of heavy smoke, assume that air quality levels are unhealthy for sensitive groups to unhealthy for all individuals,” the district warned.
 The fact that the fire broke out on a Sunday, when most of the crew was off the ship, underlines the particular and persistent threat that fire poses to warships, where even while in port, industrial work and maintenance can go on at all hours of the day, even on weekends.
 Ships like the Bonhomme Richard are often rotated through tours in Japan, where the tempo of operations is the highest in the Navy, leading to heavy wear and tear that is usually addressed with lengthy periods in a dry dock upon their return to the United States. Before the fire, the Bonhomme Richard was completing an extended shipyard period ahead of its next deployment. Now the ship faces an uncertain future.
 The Navy cannot even begin to know the scope of the work ahead until the last fire is extinguished and all of the red-hot steel has cooled. Firefighters wearing air tanks have returned to the inside of the ship after the Bonhomme Richard was completely evacuated on Sunday afternoon.
 Even in cases of catastrophic damage, however, the Navy has a long history of bringing ships back to life.
 In 1987, the U.S.S. Stark was saved after being  hit by two Iraqi missiles and returned to service. The next year, the frigate U.S.S. Samuel B. Roberts survived after  striking a naval mine in the Persian Gulf.
 During the Persian Gulf war in 1991, the cruiser U.S.S. Princeton and the amphibious ship U.S.S. Tripoli both  struck naval mines; they later returned to duty.
 And in 2000, the U.S.S. Cole was kept afloat after  being attacked by a suicide boat packed with explosives that tore a 30-foot wide hole in its hull. It was placed onto a heavy-lift ship that transported it back to the United States for repairs before it was later returned to service.
 Similar lift ships were used to carry  two other destroyers in recent years after collisions at sea. One of them, the U.S.S. Fitzgerald, was moored just across the pier from the Bonhomme Richard and ultimately moved by tugboats after being overwhelmed by smoke billowing from the larger ship.
title: 海军军舰仍在燃烧，现在正向一侧倾斜
title_en: Navy Warship Is Still Ablaze, and Now Tilting to One Side

这家美国零售集团表示，随着世界第二大互联网市场开始从新冠肺炎危机中复苏，新的资本将帮助Flipkart进一步发展其在印度的电子商务市场。两年前，Flipkart的估值为220亿美元。
Flipkart首席执行官卡利安·克里希纳穆尔蒂(Kalyan Krishnamurthy)在一份声明中表示：“我们感谢股东的大力支持，因为我们在这些充满挑战的时期继续建设我们的平台，并服务于印度消费者日益增长的需求。”
自从沃尔玛对Flipkart进行初始投资以来，我们通过技术、伙伴关系和新服务大大扩大了我们的服务范围。今天，我们在电子和时尚领域处于领先地位，并正在迅速加快在其他一般商品类别和杂货领域的份额，同时为我们的客户提供越来越无缝的支付和送货选择。我们将继续创新，把下一个2亿印度购物者带到网上。“他补充说。
在印度与亚马逊竞争的Flipkart表示，在截至今年3月的财年中，其月度活跃客户数量同比激增45%，这些客户的交易量增加了30%。这家成立13年的公司表示，最近每月的访问量超过15亿次。
新的注资正值一家新的、强大的参与者开始进入印度电商市场之际。JioMart是印度最大的零售连锁店Reliance Retail和印度最大的电信运营商Jio Platforms的合资企业，今年早些时候在孟买选定的郊区推出，此后已扩展到全国200多个城镇。
note_en:
The American retail group said the fresh capital would help Flipkart, which was valued at $22 billion two years ago, further grow its e-commerce marketplace in India as the world’s second largest internet market begins to recover from Covid-19 crisis.
 “We’re grateful for the strong backing of our shareholders as we continue to build our platform and serve the growing needs of Indian consumers during these challenging times,” said Flipkart chief executive Kalyan Krishnamurthy in a statement.
  “Since Walmart’s initial investment in Flipkart, we have greatly expanded our offer through technology, partnerships and new services. Today, we lead in electronics and fashion, and are rapidly accelerating share in other general merchandise categories and grocery, all while providing increasingly seamless payment and delivery options for our customers. We will continue innovating to bring the next 200 million Indian shoppers online,” he added.
 Flipkart, which competes with Amazon in India, said its monthly active customers figure surged 45% in the financial year that ended in March this year, compared to the year before, and these customers are making 30% more transactions. The 13-year-old firm said it recently surpassed 1.5 billion visits per month.
 The new capital infusion comes at a time when a new, powerful player has started to make a play in the Indian e-commerce market. JioMart, a joint venture between Reliance Retail (India’s largest retail chain) and Jio Platforms (India’s largest telecom operator), launched earlier this year in select sub urban areas of Mumbai and has since expanded to more than 200 cities and towns across the nation.
title: 沃尔玛向印度Flipkart额外投资12亿美元增持股份
title_en: Walmart invests additional $1.2 billion in India’s Flipkart to increase stake

由杰夫·莫林(CEO)和托德·达格雷斯(Todd Dagres)共同创立的Liteboxer今天将推出一个全新搭建的平台，让用户有机会在家里进行令人兴奋的拳击锻炼。
星火资本(Spark Capital)联合创始人兼普通合伙人达格雷斯(Dagres)在一家健身房爱上了拳击教练后，萌生了创建Liteboxer的想法。Dagres说，在家里背着沉重的袋子复制这种锻炼是令人难以置信的困难。
达格雷斯说：“我一周只能和教练锻炼一次，而且我们都知道，一周锻炼一次是不够的。”“所以我试着在家里重复锻炼，买了一个很重的袋子。对我来说，这只是一次令人沮丧的经历。太无聊了。如果我能做到这一点，那将是一次很好的有氧运动。它就是单调乏味，令人不快。袋子就放在那里，它对你没有任何影响，所以你会觉得用拳头砸它有种罪恶感。“。
沉重的袋子也需要悬挂系统，而且经常会与沙子或水提供的重量弄得一团糟。Liteboxer是沉重包的独立替代品，它配备了照明系统，引导用户完成他们选择的锻炼。
灯光不是随机的。Liteboxer与专业拳击教练合作，通过一款可以在iOS或Android上运行的应用程序，提供由灯光和语音/音乐引导的指导锻炼。该装置还配备了力传感器，为用户提供有关力量、时间和锻炼准确性的指标，使他们能够看到自己随着时间的推移而取得的进展。
与peloton或其他联网健身设备一样，Liteboxer需要支付硬件的前期成本和访问内容的经常性成本。现在可以预购的独立式单元售价为1,495美元，而自助套餐的订阅费为29美元/月。
在内容端有几种不同的模式，包括一个训练营，让用户熟悉系统及其工作原理。在光谱的另一端，有专家指导的拳击课程。但是，也许最有趣的是，在这两者之间有一个级别，称为快速播放(Quick Play)。Quick Play使用获得专利的节奏技术来匹配系统，使其与歌曲的节拍保持一致。这让用户可以灵活地说他们想去一轮、三轮或五轮，只需随着音乐的节拍进行装箱，当涉及到锻炼的一般长度时，允许进行一些定制。
此外，Liteboxer提供了挑战朋友进行与你相同的锻炼的能力，让你可以并排看到结果。
对于Dagres和Morin来说，最重要的目标是在用户中培养一种习惯。
Dagres说：“你不仅会变得更强壮，而且这也是一种精神锻炼和巨大的压力释放器。”“它有助于提高你的心智敏锐度，而且它不仅仅是一种有氧机类型的东西。”所以我们希望人们对此习以为常。“。
他补充说，我们的目标是每周三次在该平台上获得用户，他说，按照这个时间表，你可以在几个月内获得大量的结果。然而，交付这些结果的关键是习惯性的参与。Dagres解释说，以音乐为基础的轮次，与教练的升级，以及社会挑战都是鼓励这种习惯的因素。
Liteboxer总共筹集了600万美元的资金，其中200万美元来自朋友和家人的一轮融资，400万美元来自威尔风险投资公司(Will Ventures)领导的种子轮(该基金由前NFL球员以赛亚·卡西文斯基领导)。其他投资者包括猛禽资本(Raptor Capital)和坎罗斯资本(Camros Capital)。
note_en:
Cofounded by Jeff Morin (CEO) and  Todd Dagres   (Chairman),  Liteboxer is launching today with a platform that was built from the ground up to give users the chance to get an exciting boxing workout at home.
 Dagres, cofounder and General Partner at Spark Capital, came up with the idea for  Liteboxer   after falling in love with boxing with a trainer in a gym. It was incredibly difficult to replicate that workout at home with a heavy bag, said Dagres.
  “I only was able to work out with a trainer once a week, and once a week is not good enough as we all know,” said Dagres. “So I tried to replicate the workout at home and I bought a heavy bag. It was just a dismal experience for me. It was so boring. If I could get myself to do it, it was a good cardio workout. It just was tedious and unpleasant. The bag is just sitting there, and it’s not doing anything to you so you kind of feel guilty punching it.”
 Heavy bags also require a suspension system, and can often be messy with weight provided by sand or water. Liteboxer is a free-standing replacement for the heavy bag, which is equipped with a lighting system that guides the user through a workout of their choice.
 The lights aren’t random. Liteboxer works with expert boxing trainers to provide a guided workout, which is led both by the lights and by voice/music via an app that can run on iOS or Android. The unit is also equipped with force sensors to give users metrics around force, timing, and accuracy on their workout, allowing them to see their progress over time.
 Like Peloton or other connected fitness devices, Liteboxer comes at an up-front cost for the hardware and a recurring cost for access to the content. The free-standing unit, which is available for pre-order now, costs $1,495, while the all-you-can-eat subscription costs $29/month.
 There are several different modes on the content side, including a Training Camp that gets users familiar with the system and how it works. On the other end of the spectrum, there are expert-guided boxing sessions. But, perhaps most interestingly, there is a level in between the two called Quick Play. Quick Play uses a patented Rhythm Technology to match the system up to the beat of a song. This gives the user the flexibility to say they’d like to go for one or three or five rounds, and simply box along with the beat of the music, allowing for a bit of customization when it comes to the general length of a workout.
 Plus, Liteboxer offers the ability to challenge friends to the same workout as you, allowing you to see the results side by side.
  The most important goal for Dagres and Morin is to create a habit among users.
 “Not only do you get stronger, but this is also a mental exercise and a huge stress reliever,” said Dagres. “It helps with your mental acuity, and it’s more than just a cardio machine type of thing. So we want people to be habitual about it.”
 He added that the goal is to get users on the platform three times a week, saying that you can get massive results in a few months on that schedule. The key to delivering those results, however, is habitual engagement. Dagres explained that the music-based rounds, the leveling up with trainers, and the social challenges are elements that encourage that habit.
 Liteboxer has raised a total of $6 million in funding, with $2 million coming from a friends and family round, and $4 million coming from a seed round led by Will Ventures (the fund led by former NFL player Isaiah Kacyvenski). Other investors include Raptor Capital and Camros Capital.
title: 拳击手Liteboxer进入拳击场
title_en: Liteboxer, the Peloton for boxing, enters the ring

这轮融资由Apax Partners的增长股权团队Apax Digital提供咨询的基金牵头，现有投资者Digital+Partners参与其中。这使得该公司到目前为止的总募集资金达到1.3亿美元。
Pricefx表示，它将利用这笔新资金进一步扩张，并巩固其在SaaS定价行业的“领先地位”。它还将投资于产品创新，扩大其合作伙伴生态系统，并考虑进行战略收购。
Pricefx成立于2011年，为任何规模的企业提供模块化的SaaS解决方案，用于价格优化管理(PO&amp；M)和配置价格报价(CPQ)。
定价优化软件通常帮助公司通过大量不断变化的数据和变量准确定义商品价格。这可能包括客户调查数据和细分市场、竞争对手数据、运营成本、库存以及历史价格和销售额等。
CPQ软件将这些变量汇总在一起，从而使公司能够以最优的方式配置产品或服务(即捆绑销售、追加销售等)，并根据成本、竞争和当地经济因素对其定价。
今年5月，Pricefx收购了法国市场领军企业Brennus Analytics，后者将人工智能功能添加到其定价优化解决方案中。
为此，Pricefx指出，自从流行病措施迫使公司“迅速应对”数字化转型，并正在寻找在经济低迷时期变得更具竞争力的方法以来，SaaS解决方案和定价软件尤其面临着越来越大的需求。因此，Pricefx表示，自今年3月以来，它已经签署了十几项新的客户协议。
Pricefx首席执行官兼联合创始人Marcin Cichon在一份声明中表示：“定价正被认为是全球企业的一项关键能力，Pricefx正在引领一种基于SaaS的方法，”Pricefx的首席执行官兼联合创始人马尔辛·西雄(Marcin Cichon)在一份声明中表示。Apax的这项投资证实了Pricefx的韧性、扩大客户基础和创新的能力-即使在充满挑战的时期也是如此。这项投资将进一步推动我们的全球商业战略和积极的产品创新，通过吸引关键人才，扩大我们日益增长的合作伙伴生态系统，并实现进一步的潜在战略收购-所有这些都是为了追求我们的使命，即尽可能简单有效地为尽可能多的公司提供定价解决方案。“
note_en:
The round was led by funds advised by Apax Digital, the growth equity team of  Apax Partners,   with participation from  existing investor Digital+ Partners. It brings the company’s total raise to date to $130 million.
 Pricefx says it will use the new funding for further expansion and to consolidate its “leadership position” within the SaaS pricing industry. It will also invest in product innovation, extend its partner ecosystem, and consider making strategic acquisitions.
  Founded in 2011, Pricefx provides a modular SaaS solution for price optimisation management (PO&M) and configure-price-quote (CPQ) for enterprises of any size.
 Pricing optimisation software typically helps companies accurately define the price of goods across a vast and constantly changing spectrum of data and variables. This can include things like customer survey data and segments, competitor data, operating costs, inventories, and historic prices and sales.
 CPQ software aggregates these variables, thus enabling companies to configure products or services in the most optimal way (i.e. bundling, up-sells, etc.), and price them according to costs, competition and local economic factors.
 In May, Pricefx acquired French market leader Brennus Analytics, which adding AI capabilities to its pricing optimization solution.
 To that end, Pricefx notes that SaaS solutions and pricing software in particular has faced increased demand since pandemic measures forced companies to “rapidly address” digital transformation and are looking for ways to become more competitive in a downturn. As a result, Pricefx says it has signed more than a dozen new customer deals since March of this year.
 “Pricing is being recognized as a critical competency for global enterprises and Pricefx is leading the way for a SaaS-based approach,” says Marcin Cichon, CEO and co-founder of Pricefx, in a statement. “This investment from Apax confirms Pricefx’s resilience, ability to grow its customer base, and innovate – even during challenging times. This investment will further power our global commercial strategy and aggressive product innovation, by attracting critical talent, expanding our growing ecosystem of partners, and enabling further potential strategic acquisitions – all in pursuit of our mission to bring pricing solutions to as many companies as we can, as simply and effectively as possible”.
title: Pricefx为其基于云的定价软件C系列筹集6500万美元
title_en: Pricefx raises $65M Series C for its cloud-based pricing software

你的车抛锚从来都不是一件令人愉快的事，但当外面气温达到128度时，情况可能会更糟。
死亡谷国家公园周一表示，周日，一些倒霉的游客遇到了汽车故障，因为极端高温导致他们的引擎熄火。
死亡谷国家公园官员在脸书上表示：“昨天的高温导致公园内至少有三辆车因发动机过热而抛锚，如果乘客滞留在这种没有空调的气候中，发动机过热可能很快就会变成致命的。”幸运的是，护林员可以做出反应，没有重大医疗事故的报道。
公园表示，周末死亡谷的高温几乎创下纪录。官方气温达到128度，上一次出现是在2013年。
国家公园管理局表示，死亡谷是有记录以来最热温度的所在地。根据NPS的数据，1913年7月10日，炉子溪的温度是134华氏度。
NPS说：“在创纪录的热浪达到顶峰期间，连续五天达到了129华氏度(54摄氏度)或更高。”“死亡谷保持着地球上最热地方的记录。”
NPS说，公园里稀疏的植物覆盖帮助太阳加热沙漠表面，这些热量从岩石和土壤中辐射出来，变得“被困在山谷的死亡中”。
据NPS报道，“死亡谷的深度和形状影响了它的夏季温度。”山谷是一个低于海平面282英尺(86米)的狭长盆地，但被高耸陡峭的山脉围住。
note_en:
Having your car break down is never pleasant, but it’s probably much worse when it’s 128 degrees outside.
 Some unlucky visitors at  Death Valley National Park ran into car trouble Sunday when the extreme heat caused their engines to give out, the park said Monday.
 “Yesterday’s excessive heat caused at least three vehicles in the park to break down from overheated engines, which can quickly turn fatal if passengers are stranded in this climate without air conditioning,” Death Valley National Park officials said on Facebook. “Fortunately, rangers were available to respond and no major medical incidents were reported.”
  The heat at Death Valley over the weekend nearly hit a record, the park said. Official temperatures reached 128 degrees, which was last seen in 2013.
 Death Valley is home of the  hottest temperature ever recorded, the National Park Service said. On July 10, 1913, it was 134 degrees Fahrenheit at Furnace Creek, according to NPS.
 “During the heatwave that peaked with that record, five consecutive days reached 129° F (54°C) or above,” NPS said. “Death Valley holds the record for the hottest place on earth.”
 The sparse plant coverage in the park helps the sun heat up the desert surface, and that heat radiates from rocks and soil, becoming “trapped in the valley’s death,” NPS said.
 “The depth and shape of Death Valley influence its summer temperatures,” according to NPS. “The valley is a long, narrow basin 282 feet (86 m) below sea level, yet is walled by high, steep mountain ranges.”
title: 死亡谷国家公园太热了，汽车都抛锚了
title_en: It’s so hot at Death Valley National Park that cars are breaking down

编者按：这是“金钱星球”时事通讯的节选。你可以在这里报名。
银行和自助洗衣店争先恐后。拱廊和口香糖售货机运营商正在为最坏的情况做准备。杂货店正在把价格四舍五入到偶数美元，或者干脆拒绝现金。硬币短缺的幽灵无处不在。
怪新冠肺炎吧。美国造币厂今年春天削减了硬币产量，以保护工人的安全。与此同时，经济不景气。随着经济的关闭，硬币在经济中的流动…。美联储(Fed)主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔(Jerome Powell)上个月解释说，有点停了。钱币闲置在关闭的商店、收银机和人们的家中，而且它们无法运往需要它们做生意的银行和公司。
硬币短缺可能是一场长期运动的战斗口号，近年来这场运动已经失去了动力：杀死一分钱！去年，美国造币厂生产的硬币中几乎有60%是便士。60%。它赚了超过70亿便士。70亿美元。这是大量的人力，可以用来制造我们真正需要的硬币。
这一便士基本上一文不值。实际上，它比一文不值还要糟糕。美国政府生产每一分钱的成本约为2美分。便士甚至不值得我们浪费时间。维克森林大学(Wake Forest University)经济学家罗伯特·惠普斯(Robert Whaples)计算出，典型的美国工人每两秒赚一便士。我们大多数人摸索零钱或从地上捡起一便士的时间超过两秒钟，这就解释了为什么地上有这么多便士。钱应该是交换的媒介，而不是沉重的负担。
吉姆·科尔贝(Jim Kolbe)花了20年时间试图扼杀这一便士。他是来自亚利桑那州的前共和党国会议员，他在20世纪80年代末开始了他的斗争。起初，把我们从一分钱中解放出来并不是一种高尚的追求。他说，坦率地说，他首先想帮助亚利桑那州的铜业，摆脱纸币，用铜币美元取而代之。他看到民意调查显示，有广泛的支持扼杀便士，所以他和他的同事们将新铜币美元的想法与扼杀便士的想法捆绑在一起。(这一便士主要由锌制成，而不是铜。)。
但随着时间的推移，科尔比说，他提出的扼杀便士的建议不仅仅是帮助铜业的一种方式。他说，这是一项合乎逻辑的改革，本可以为美国政府节省一大笔钱。
科尔比说，到了20世纪90年代，他提出了新的立法，以便在国会的每一届新会议上省钱。但他一直面临阻力-例如，来自当时众议院议长丹尼斯·哈斯特尔特(Dennis Hastert)的阻力，哈斯特尔特代表亚伯拉罕·林肯(Abraham Lincoln)的家乡伊利诺伊州的一个选区。当然，林肯手头拮据，科尔比说，事实证明这是一个主要的障碍。特殊利益集团也是如此，比如锌矿商和供应铸造一便士的毛坯的公司。
一分钱的捍卫者最有力的论据是，取消它会损害消费者的利益。所有那些9.99美元的产品？价格甚至会涨到10美元！他们称之为四舍五入税。但维克森林大学(Wake Forest University)那位研究便士的经济学家惠普斯(Whaples)对便利店进行了一项研究，发现购物的最终数字几乎是随机的，通常涉及多种产品和销售税。惠普斯说，所以如果你把它四舍五入到最近的镍币，顾客就不会被骗了。有时你会四舍五入；有时你会四舍五入。归根结底，这基本上是一场洗礼。
在美国历史上的大部分时间里，我们从来没有一枚硬币像现在这样一文不值。早在1857年，我们就废除了5美分硬币--经通货膨胀调整后，它当时的价值相当于今天的14美分。我们做得很好。前国会议员科尔比说，当他去商店，拿到一块印有亚伯·林肯面孔的无用的金属时，他和其他人一样感到困惑，因为我认为这是我们立法过程中问题的一个很好的例证。
美国造币厂去年因制造便士而亏损超过7200万美元。但这似乎没有太大的紧迫性，因为在联邦预算的宏伟计划中，这只是几分钱。我们联系了美国造币厂讨论硬币短缺的问题，其代表迈克尔·怀特(Michael White)告诉我们，在大流行的早期，美国造币厂进行了重新装备，以确保员工的安全，自6月中旬以来，美国造币厂一直在满负荷运营。但是，低迷的零售活动和硬币加工者存款的减少-比如，你知道，超市里那些将硬币兑换成纸币的机器-阻碍了硬币的流通。怀特说，自从美国造币厂为结束硬币短缺而开足马力以来，它生产的硬币中约有40%是便士。
另外，你应该听听“星球金钱”中关于这一便士的一集，一便士值多少钱？它有三个有趣的故事，包括寻找东西(任何东西！)。我们可以用1美分购买，梦想着1美分交易将如何改变互联网。

note_en:
Editor's note:  This is an excerpt of Planet Money 's newsletter. You can   sign up here.
    Banks and laundromats are scrambling. Arcades and gumball machine operators are bracing for the worst. Grocery stores are rounding their prices to even dollars or rejecting cash altogether. The specter of the  coin shortage lurks everywhere.
  Blame COVID-19. The U.S. Mint cut back on coin production this spring to keep its workers safe. Meanwhile, the economy is constipated. "With the closure of the economy, the flow of coins through the economy has ... kind of stopped," explained Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell last month. Coins sit idle in closed stores' cash registers and people's homes, and they're not making it to the banks and companies that need them for business.
  The coin shortage could be a rallying cry for a long-running movement that has lost steam in recent years: Kill the penny! Last year,  almost 60% of the coins that the U.S. Mint churned out were pennies.  60 percent. It made  more than 7 billion pennies.  Seven billion. That's a lot of manpower that could be used toward making coins we actually need.
  The penny is basically worthless. Actually, it's worse than worthless. It costs the U.S. government  about 2 cents to produce every penny. Pennies aren't even worth our time. Wake Forest University economist Robert Whaples has calculated that the typical American worker earns a penny every two seconds. It takes most of us more than two seconds to fumble around with change or pick a penny off the ground, which explains why there are so many pennies on the ground. Money is supposed to be the medium of exchange, not dead weight.
    Jim Kolbe spent two decades trying to kill the penny. He's a former Republican congressman from Arizona, and he began his fight in the late 1980s. Initially, it was not a noble-minded quest to free us of the penny. He says, frankly, that he first wanted to help the copper industry in Arizona by getting rid of the paper dollar and replacing it with a copper-coin dollar. He saw polling that showed there was widespread support for killing the penny, so he and his colleagues bundled the idea of a new copper-coin dollar with the idea of killing the penny. (The penny is made mostly of zinc, not copper.)
    But over time, Kolbe says, his proposal to kill the penny became more than just a way to help the copper industry. "It was a logical reform that would have saved the U.S. government a lot of money," he says.
  By the 1990s, Kolbe says, he was introducing new legislation to kill the penny with every new session of Congress. But he kept facing resistance — for example, from the speaker of the House at the time, Dennis Hastert, who represented a district in Illinois, the home state of Abraham Lincoln. Lincoln, of course, is on the penny, and Kolbe says that proved to be a major roadblock. So were special interests such as zinc miners and the company that supplies the "penny blanks" used to mint the penny.
  Penny defenders' strongest argument was that eliminating it would hurt consumers. All those $9.99 products? The prices would be jacked up to an even $10! They called it the "rounding tax." But Whaples, that penny-researching economist at Wake Forest University, conducted  a study of convenience stores and found that the final digit of purchases, which usually involve multiple products and a sales tax, was pretty much random. "And so if you round it to the nearest nickel, the customer wouldn't get gouged," Whaples says. Sometimes you'd round up; other times you'd round down. In the end, it would basically be a wash.
  For most of U.S. history, we never had a coin as worthless as the penny is now. Back in 1857, we killed the half-cent coin — which, when adjusted for inflation, was as valuable then as about 14 cents is today. And we did just fine. Kolbe, the former congressman, says that when he goes to the store and is handed a useless hunk of metal with Abe Lincoln's face on it, he is "bemused as much as anything because I think it's a good illustration of the problems in our legislative process."
  The U.S. Mint lost  over $72 million making pennies last year. But there doesn't seem to be much urgency about this because in the grand scheme of the federal budget, it's just pennies. We reached out to the U.S. Mint to discuss the coin shortage, and its representative Michael White told us that after retooling to keep its employees safe during the early part of the pandemic, the U.S. Mint has been operating at full capacity since mid-June. But depressed retail activity and reduced deposits by coin processors — like, you know, those machines at the supermarket that exchange your coins for bills — have hampered coin circulation. Since the U.S. Mint went into overdrive to end the coin shortage, White says, about 40% of the coins that it has produced have been pennies.
  P.S. You should listen to a great Planet Money  episode about the penny, "  What's A Penny Worth? " It has three fun stories, including a quest to find something (anything!) we could purchase with 1 cent and the dream of how 1-cent transactions could transform the Internet.

title: 是时候杀了那便士了吗？
title_en: Is It Time to Kill the Penny?
